mapping.a_element: a strongly sheared many-particle fluid studied by molecular/computer simulation
mapping.b_element: a strongly sheared many-particle fluid studied by computer experiment

brief_justification: The only genuine overlap is the shared object of study (a sheared many-particle fluid probed by simulation); the machinery differs entirely: paper A uses molecular-dynamics of polymer-brush conformations to explain hydrodynamic flow inversion and a Navier slip condition, whereas paper B applies the Sinai-Ruelle-Bowen / Ruelle turbulence-principle formalism of dynamical-systems ergodic theory to predict nonequilibrium-stationary-state statistics, so no role-for-role mapping of governing mechanisms holds.
